session.objective: Build defensive infrastructure that prevents template/agent edits from drifting away from hand-maintained install copies under .claude/agents/ and .github/agents/. Add validator, tests, pre-push guard, and CI gate. Refs #2094.

constraintsRead.Evidence: PROJECT-CONSTRAINTS via .agents/governance referenced in AGENTS.md; ADR-035 (exit codes), REQ-003-010 (.claude no-write invariant) read inline

memoriesLoaded.Evidence: Loaded build pipeline architecture from build/scripts/build_all.py and templates/platforms/copilot-cli.yaml directly (Serena symbol search not required for this scope)

markdownLintRun.Evidence: npx markdownlint-cli2 not invoked on .py files (correct, .py are not markdown); pre_pr.py markdown step covers all repo .md and only pre-existing issues in src/copilot-cli/skills/* fail, unrelated to this change

retrospectiveInvoked.Evidence: Retrospective deferred; the root-cause analysis is captured in the issue #2094 body which is the canonical document for this drift class

workLog.action: Mapped the canonical-vs-install architecture. templates/agents/*.shared.md drives src/claude/, src/copilot-cli/agents/, src/vs-code-agents/ via build/generate_agents.py. .claude/agents/ and .github/agents/ are hand-maintained self-host copies; REQ-003-010 in build_all.py forbids the generator from writing under .claude/.

Catalogued landed and open drift. Landed in main: PR #2087 (qa) and PR #2083 (orchestrator) skipped .claude/agents/ updates; analyst and critic earlier rewrites missed .github/agents/ siblings. Open: PR #2089 (architect) and PR #2084 (implementer) currently only touch templates+src.

Implemented validate_install_parity.py with two parity groups: SHARED_AGENT (6 siblings) and RULE (3 siblings). Skills/commands/hooks/lib intentionally excluded; they are auto-generated by build_all and covered by build_all --check.

Wrote 22 unit tests. First run surfaced two bugs: lstrip("./") was stripping the leading dot in .claude/ and .github/ paths, and the AGENTS_BLOCKLIST did not contain the bare stem captured by the regex group. Fixed both, all tests green.

Built pre-push guard on push_guard_base; lazy imports the validator from build/scripts/ so the guard fails open on consumer-repo checkouts without the build tree. Wired into .claude/settings.json under the git push matcher.

Added an Install-parity check step to validate-generated-agents.yml so every PR that touches the relevant trees gates on the validator. Used env: PR_BASE_REF rather than direct ${{ }} interpolation in run: per workflow security guidance.
Registered the validator in scripts/validation/pre_pr.py so contributors get the same gate locally. Always-on (not quick-skip) because the run cost is 50 ms.

Verified validator catches real drift: python3 build/scripts/validate_install_parity.py --base main~10 flags analyst, critic (missing .github/agents/), orchestrator (missing .claude/, .github/, src/claude/), and qa (missing .claude/, .github/). Verified branch-local run is clean.
Created issue #2094 documenting the drift class, evidence, root cause, and the defenses introduced here. Referenced from the upcoming PR.

PR #2095 feedback cycle: addressed 16 bot threads. Clusters: (A) hook include_deletions=True so deletion-only parity breaks reach the validator; (B) _is_shared_agent_group accepts touched_set so template deletions still bind the parity contract (added 2 regression tests); (C) explicit --base no longer falls back to origin/main (added 2 tests asserting explicit/default behavior); (D) pre_pr.py passes --base from _resolve_branch_base_ref and fails closed when validator missing; (E) extracted CI bash logic into scripts/validation/run_install_parity_ci.py per ADR-006, fetch-depth=0 in checkout, PUSH_BEFORE_SHA fallback for push runs; (F) install-parity guard emits EVENT lines for every fail-open path (no silent degradation); (G) marketplace.json hook count 39 -> 37 to match src/copilot-cli/hooks/hooks.json. Tests: pytest tests/build_scripts tests/hooks tests/validation: 1251 passed in 5.0s.

nextSteps: Open the PR for #2094 referencing this session log.
Follow-up PR: regenerate .claude/agents/qa.md and .claude/agents/orchestrator.md from templates/ to fix the landed drift in main.
Post review comments on open PRs #2089 (architect) and #2084 (implementer) asking the authors to include the missing .claude/agents/ and .github/agents/ siblings.
Consider an ADR amendment for REQ-003-010 that explicitly documents the install-parity boundary so the rule is not re-discovered next time.
